item.title: Top 7 Must-Try Korean Street Foods You Can’t Miss in Seoul
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
🍢 Discover the Soul of Seoul Through Its Street Food

Seoul’s street food scene is a paradise for food lovers. From sweet treats to spicy snacks, every corner offers something mouthwatering. Whether you’re exploring Myeongdong, Hongdae, or Gwangjang Market, here are 7 must-try Korean street foods you can’t miss during your trip.

1️⃣ Tteokbokki (Spicy Rice Cakes)

These chewy rice cakes simmered in spicy gochujang sauce are a national favorite. The perfect balance of sweet, spicy, and savory makes tteokbokki an irresistible comfort food.

Where to try: Sindang-dong Tteokbokki Town or Myeongdong street stalls.

2️⃣ Hotteok (Sweet Syrup-Filled Pancakes)

A crispy pancake stuffed with brown sugar, cinnamon, and crushed nuts. Hotteok is especially popular in winter — one bite and you’ll be hooked!

Tip: Try green tea or matcha versions at Insadong’s street markets.

3️⃣ Eomuk (Fish Cake Skewers)

Served on skewers with warm broth, eomuk is the ultimate comfort food during cold weather. The broth is free and refilled as much as you like!

Best spots: Gwangjang Market and Namdaemun Market.

4️⃣ Gimbap (Korean Seaweed Rice Rolls)

Often called the Korean version of sushi, gimbap is filled with rice, vegetables, egg, and ham — wrapped in seaweed. Portable, healthy, and delicious.

Try it at: Local convenience stores or Gwangjang Market’s famous gimbap stalls.

5️⃣ Odeng Soup & Tangsuyuk Combo

Pair your fish cake soup with crispy tangsuyuk (sweet and sour pork). It’s a Korean-Chinese favorite fusion combo you’ll find near subway stations and markets.

6️⃣ Tornado Potato

Spiral-cut potato deep-fried on a stick — crunchy, salty, and so fun to eat! Choose your flavor: cheese, onion, or spicy powder for an extra kick.

7️⃣ Bungeoppang (Fish-Shaped Pastry)

This adorable fish-shaped pastry is filled with sweet red bean, custard, or Nutella. Perfect snack while strolling through winter streets.

Hot tip: Pair it with hot corn tea or sweet milk for the ultimate cozy combo.

💡 Final Thoughts

Seoul’s street food isn’t just about eating — it’s about experiencing Korean culture with every bite. Each dish tells a story of flavor, community, and comfort. So next time you’re in Seoul, skip the fancy restaurants and follow the mouthwatering smell of sizzling street stalls!
item.excerpt:encoded: Explore Seoul’s vibrant street food culture with this guide to 7 must-try Korean snacks — from spicy tteokbokki to sweet hotteok and crispy tornado potatoes.

item.title: 7 Must-Try Korean Convenience Store Foods
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
Introduction

Korean convenience stores like CU, GS25, and 7-Eleven are famous for offering delicious, affordable, and surprisingly creative foods. Whether you're traveling in Korea or living here, these 7 must-try items represent everything people love about K-convenience culture in 2025.

1. Triangle Kimbap (Samgak Kimbap)

This classic on-the-go snack is still the No.1 best seller. Popular flavors include Tuna Mayo, Spicy Pork, and Kimchi Fried Rice. Perfect for a quick breakfast or a budget-friendly meal.

Best picks

  • GS25 – “Double Mayo Tuna”
  • CU – “Spam Mayo with Cheese”

2. Instant Ramen Cups

Korean cup ramen is globally loved for its rich broth and chewy noodles. Most stores also provide hot water stations, making it easy to enjoy ramen on the spot.

Trending flavors

  • Buldak Spicy Chicken
  • Jin Ramen Mild
  • Yukgaejang Cup

3. Hot Bar (Eomuk & Sausage Sticks)

Convenience store hot bars are convenient, warm, and filling. You can find sausages, cheese-filled fish cakes, or spicy skewers freshly heated in portable warmers.

4. Korean Lunch Boxes (Dosirak)

Affordable meal boxes containing rice, meat, and various sides. 2025 versions feature premium menus like beef bulgogi or pork cutlet sets.

Why you should try it

  • Balanced meal under 6,000 KRW
  • A wide variety including vegetarian options

5. Banana Milk

A nostalgic Korean drink loved for decades. You’ll find it in every convenience store—sweet, smooth, and perfectly balanced.

6. Ice Cream (Melona, World Cone, More)

From classic Melona to modern premium cones, Korean ice cream is cheap, refreshing, and iconic.

7. Ready-to-Drink Coffee

Cafés are everywhere in Korea, but convenience store iced lattes and Americanos offer solid taste at a lower price.

Final Tips for Convenience Store Eating in Korea

  • Look for seasonal or limited-edition flavors.
  • Many stores offer microwave stations for heating meals.
  • Try the “meal combo” deals to save money.

Conclusion

Korean convenience stores continue to evolve with new flavors and innovative food combinations. If you're visiting Korea in 2025, don’t miss these 7 must-try items. They’re delicious, inexpensive, and full of Korean character.
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover the top 7 Korean convenience store foods you must try in 2025 — from triangle kimbap to cup ramen, hot bars, lunch boxes, and more.

item.title: How to Use Korean Subway & Transportation (2025 Guide: T-money, Naver Map & KakaoMap)
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
🚇 Your Complete 2025 Guide to Using Korean Subway & Transportation

South Korea has one of the most efficient and foreigner-friendly transportation systems in the world. With a T-money card, Naver Map, and KakaoMap, navigating the subway, buses, and taxis becomes incredibly simple — even for first-time visitors.

Read also: 7 Must-Try Korean Convenience Store Foods

💳 1️⃣ What Is T-money & Why You Need It

T-money is Korea’s universal transportation card used for:

  • Subways
  • Buses
  • Taxis
  • Convenience stores (sometimes)

📍 Where to Buy

  • GS25, CU, 7-Eleven
  • Airport convenience stores (Incheon / Gimpo)
  • Subway station vending machines

🔄 How to Recharge

  • Subway ticket machine (cash/card)
  • Convenience stores
  • T-money app (Android only)

Why it’s recommended: Automatically gives transfer discounts + saves time.

🚇 2️⃣ How to Use the Korean Subway System

The Korean subway is clean, fast, and fully bilingual with English signs.

Step-by-Step Subway Guide

  1. Tap your T-money card at the entrance gate.
  2. Check the line color and platform number.
  3. Transfer by following the clear direction signs.
  4. Tap again when exiting the station.

Helpful Tips

  • Use priority seats only if necessary — it’s strictly respected in Korea.
  • Many stations have elevators and escalators for luggage.
  • Trains run until around midnight, depending on the city.

🚌 3️⃣ Using Korean Buses

Buses are color-coded and highly efficient. All accept T-money.

How to Ride a Bus

  1. Check bus arrival times on Naver Map or KakaoMap.
  2. Tap your T-money when boarding.
  3. Press the stop button before getting off.
  4. Tap again at the exit.

Tip: Blue buses cover long routes, green buses run locally, and red buses connect suburbs.

🚕 4️⃣ Taxis & Airport Transportation

Taxis are affordable and easy to hail using Kakao T (English available).

Payment Options

  • T-money
  • Credit/debit card (most foreign cards accepted)
  • KakaoPay (if set up)

From the Airport

  • AREX train: Fastest to Seoul Station
  • Airport limousine bus: Best for luggage
  • Taxi: Comfortable but more expensive

🗺️ 5️⃣ How to Use Naver Map

Naver Map is the most accurate navigation tool in Korea.

How to Navigate

  1. Search your destination in English or Korean.
  2. Choose subway, bus, taxi, or walking.
  3. Follow step-by-step instructions including transfers.

Tip: Naver Map is best for subways & walking routes.

📍 6️⃣ How to Use KakaoMap

KakaoMap provides fast and precise real-time data.

Why Travelers Love It

  • Strong live bus tracking
  • More detailed map layers
  • Great for finding restaurants and shops

💬 Frequently Asked Questions

1. Do I really need a T-money card?

Yes — it’s cheaper, faster, and gives transfer discounts. Single tickets are slower and cost more.

2. Does Google Maps work in Korea?

No. Google Maps cannot provide accurate navigation in Korea. Use Naver Map or KakaoMap instead.

3. Can I use a foreign credit card?

Yes! Most taxis, convenience stores, and subway machines accept Visa, Mastercard, and AmEx.

4. Is public transportation safe at night?

Yes. Korea’s subway and buses are extremely safe with strong CCTV coverage.

5. Can I use Apple Pay or Google Pay?

Some taxis accept Apple Pay, but not all. Always have a T-money card as backup.

✨ Final Tips

With a T-money card and one of the major navigation apps, getting around Korea becomes simple even for beginners. Whether you're visiting Seoul, Busan, or Jeju, this guide will help you travel comfortably and confidently.
item.excerpt:encoded: A complete 2025 guide on using Korea’s subway, transportation system, T-money cards, Naver Map, and KakaoMap — perfect for first-time travelers.

item.title: Best Ski Resorts Near Seoul for 2025-2026 Winter: The Ultimate Guide
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
Are you planning a winter trip to Korea? Finding the best ski resorts near Seoul for the 2025-2026 winter season is essential for a perfect itinerary. South Korea offers world-class skiing facilities, and many are located just 40 to 90 minutes away from the capital city.

This guide covers the top ski resorts accessible from Seoul, including Vivaldi Park, Konjiam Resort, and Elysian Gangchon, along with transportation tips.

Read also: Top 10 Winter Festivals in Korea You Must Visit in 2025

Why Choose Ski Resorts Near Seoul?

Ski resorts in Gyeonggi-do and nearby Gangwon-do offer excellent accessibility for international tourists. You can enjoy a full day of skiing without the need for overnight accommodation. Most resorts provide shuttle buses from major hubs like Myeongdong, Hongdae, and Gangnam.

Which Ski Resort is the Most Popular for Tourists?

Vivaldi Park Ski World (Hongcheon)

Vivaldi Park Ski World is arguably the most famous ski resort for international visitors. It offers frequent free shuttle buses from Seoul, making it incredibly convenient for first-timers.

  • Location: Hongcheon-gun, Gangwon-do (approx. 1.5 hours from Seoul)
  • Best Feature: "Snowy Land" – a massive snow theme park perfect for sledding and photos.
  • Tip: It can get crowded on weekends; a weekday visit is highly recommended.

What is the Closest Ski Resort to Seoul?

Konjiam Resort (Gwangju)

Konjiam Resort is the closest ski destination, located in Gwangju, Gyeonggi-do. It takes only about 40 minutes from Gangnam by car. It is famous for its premium facilities and "Me-Time Pass" system, which allows you to ski for a specific number of hours efficiently.

  • Location: Gwangju-si, Gyeonggi-do
  • Best Feature: Shortest travel time from Seoul and limited daily entry for a pleasant experience.
  • Note: Prices are slightly higher, but the proximity justifies the cost.

Can I Go Skiing by Subway in Korea?

Elysian Gangchon Resort (Chuncheon)

Yes, you can. Elysian Gangchon Resort is unique because it is accessible via the Seoul subway system (Gyeongchun Line) and the ITX-Cheongchun train.

  • Location: Chuncheon-si, Gangwon-do (approx. 1 hour via ITX)
  • Best Feature: Direct access to Baegyang-ri Station and beginner-friendly slopes.
  • Vibe: Very youthful and popular among university students and independent travelers.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

When does the 2025-2026 ski season start in Korea?

The ski season in Korea typically begins in early December 2025 and continues until early March 2026. The best snow conditions are usually found in January.

Do I need to bring my own ski gear?

No. All major resorts near Seoul offer rental services for skis, snowboards, boots, and clothing (jackets/pants). You should bring your own gloves and goggles, or purchase them at the resort.

How much is a ski lift pass in Korea?

Lift passes vary by duration, but a 4-hour pass typically costs between 60,000 KRW and 90,000 KRW (approx. $45-$70 USD) depending on the resort and discounts.
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover the best ski resorts near Seoul for the 2025-2026 winter season. A complete guide to Vivaldi Park, Konjiam, and Elysian Gangchon for an unforgettable winter day trip in Korea.

item.title: Cost of Living in Seoul vs. Busan: 2025 Real Data Comparison
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
Are you deciding where to live in South Korea? Choosing between the bustling capital, Seoul, and the coastal metropolis, Busan, is a common dilemma for expats and digital nomads. In this guide, we break down the cost of living in Seoul vs. Busan using 2025 real data to help you make an informed decision.

While Seoul offers endless career opportunities, Busan provides a more relaxed lifestyle by the ocean. But how do they compare financially?

Read also: Best Neighborhoods to Live in Seoul for Expats (2025 Guide)

Is Seoul Much More Expensive Than Busan?

Yes, generally speaking, Seoul is about 20-30% more expensive than Busan. The biggest difference lies in housing costs. While food and transportation prices are relatively similar, rent and property prices in Seoul are significantly higher.

1. Housing and Rent Costs

Housing is the most significant expense in Korea. In 2025, the gap between Seoul and Busan remains wide.

Rent in Seoul

Seoul's rental market is highly competitive. A standard one-room (studio) in popular areas like Gangnam or Mapo can cost between 800,000 KRW and 1,200,000 KRW per month, with a deposit (key money) ranging from 10 million to 20 million KRW.

Rent in Busan

Busan offers much better value. A similar studio in Seomyeon or near Gwangalli Beach typically costs 450,000 KRW to 650,000 KRW per month. You can often find larger apartments with ocean views for the price of a small shoe-box room in Seoul.

2. Food and Dining

Do you eat out often? Here is what you can expect to pay for meals.

  • Seoul: An average lunch in the business district costs around 11,000 KRW to 15,000 KRW. A coffee at a nice cafe is roughly 5,000 KRW to 7,000 KRW.
  • Busan: Local meals are slightly cheaper, averaging 9,000 KRW to 12,000 KRW. Seafood markets like Jagalchi offer fresh options at competitive prices, though tourist spots can be pricey.

3. Transportation Costs

Public transport is excellent in both cities, but there are slight differences in 2025 fares.

  • Subway (Base Fare): Seoul (~1,500 KRW) vs. Busan (~1,450 KRW).
  • Taxi (Base Fare): Seoul (4,800 KRW) vs. Busan (4,800 KRW). While base fares are similar, shorter travel distances in Busan often result in lower overall taxi bills.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

Is a salary of 3 million KRW enough to live in Seoul?

Yes, 3 million KRW is a decent entry-level salary for a single person in Seoul. After rent and taxes, you will have enough for a comfortable lifestyle, though saving large amounts might be challenging compared to living in Busan.

Which city is better for digital nomads: Seoul or Busan?

If you prefer networking and fast-paced city life, Seoul is better. However, if you prefer a work-life balance with nature and lower costs, Busan is the superior choice for 2025.

Are utilities expensive in Korea?

Utilities (electricity, gas, water, internet) for a one-person studio typically cost around 150,000 KRW to 200,000 KRW per month in both cities, depending on air conditioning and heating usage.
item.excerpt:encoded: Comparing the cost of living in Seoul vs. Busan for 2025. Discover real data on rent, food, and transport to decide which Korean city fits your budget best.

item.title: Olive Young Must-Haves 2025: The Ultimate K-Beauty Souvenir Guide
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
Are you visiting Korea in 2025? A trip to Olive Young is non-negotiable for any beauty lover. As the "Sephora of Korea," it offers an overwhelming variety of skincare and makeup products.

To help you shop smart, we have curated a list of the top Olive Young must-haves for 2025. These are the items Korean locals actually buy and love.

Read also: Top 10 Korean Sunscreens You Need to Try in 2025

What Should I Buy at Olive Young in 2025?

The trends for 2025 focus on "Glass Skin" hydration, calming ingredients like Heartleaf, and home-care devices. Here are the absolute essentials to put in your shopping basket.

1. Skincare: The Hydration & Soothing Heroes

Biodance Bio-Collagen Real Deep Mask

This mask went viral on TikTok for a reason. It starts as a white sheet and turns transparent as your skin absorbs the collagen over 3-4 hours (or overnight). It leaves your skin incredibly glowing and plump.

  • Best for: Overnight hydration and "glass skin" effect.
  • Tip: Buy the bulk box; you will regret buying just one.

Torriden Dive-In Low Molecular Hyaluronic Acid Serum

Consistently ranked #1, this serum is like a glass of water for your face. It is lightweight, non-sticky, and perfect for all skin types, including sensitive and oily skin.

  • Best for: Deep hydration without greasiness.

Anua Heartleaf 77% Soothing Toner

If you have redness or acne-prone skin, this is your holy grail. The high concentration of Heartleaf extract calms irritation instantly. It is arguably the most famous toner in Korea right now.

2. Makeup: The "Clean Girl" Aesthetic

Rom&nd Juicy Lasting Tint

No K-beauty haul is complete without a Rom&nd tint. The shade "Bare Grape" is a cool-toned MLBB (My Lips But Better) color that suits almost everyone. It leaves a glossy, tangible finish that lasts.

Clio Kill Lash Superproof Mascara

Koreans know how to make mascara that holds a curl against humidity. The Clio Kill Lash is smudge-proof, clump-free, and keeps straight lashes curled all day long.

3. Hair & Body Care

UNOVE Deep Damage Treatment EX

Transform dry, damaged hair into silk with this protein-rich treatment. It is a massive best-seller at Olive Young for restoring hair texture.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

Can I get a tax refund at Olive Young?

Yes! Most major Olive Young stores offer immediate tax refunds at the cashier if you spend over 15,000 KRW. Remember to bring your physical passport.

When is the Olive Young Big Sale in 2025?

Olive Young typically holds massive sales (up to 70% off) in early March, June, September, and December. These "Olive Young Sale" periods usually last for one week.

Are these brands cheaper in Korea?

Absolutely. Brands like Torriden, Anua, and Rom&nd are significantly cheaper in Korea compared to buying them on Amazon or global sites, especially with 1+1 deals.
item.excerpt:encoded: Discover the top Olive Young must-haves for 2025. From viral collagen masks to the best K-beauty makeup souvenirs, here is your ultimate shopping list for Korea.

item.title: Jeju Island Winter Travel Guide 2025: Hallasan Snow Hiking & Camellia Spots
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
Planning a winter escape to South Korea? Jeju Island transforms into a magical wonderland during the colder months, offering a unique blend of snow-capped mountains and vibrant floral landscapes. This Jeju Island Winter Travel Guide for 2025 will help you explore the best of what this volcanic island has to offer, from exhilarating Hallasan snow hiking to picturesque camellia spots.

Discover why Jeju is not just a summer destination but a captivating winter paradise, attracting both local and international visitors.

Read also: Top 5 Must-Try Jeju Black Pork Restaurants for 2025

Why Visit Jeju Island in Winter (2025)?

Jeju's winter charm lies in its diverse offerings. You can experience the breathtaking snowy vistas of Hallasan, then descend to warmer coastal areas to witness the vivid red camellias in full bloom. The island is also less crowded than in peak seasons, allowing for a more serene travel experience.

1. Hallasan Snow Hiking: A Winter Wonderland Experience

Is Hallasan Mountain Open for Hiking in Winter?

Yes, Hallasan Mountain is open for hiking in winter, but specific trails and conditions apply. For the 2025 season, the most popular routes for reaching the summit (Baengnokdam Crater Lake) are the Seongpanak Trail and Gwanneumsan Trail. Both require prior reservation.

  • Seongpanak Trail: (~9.6 km, 5 hours one way to summit) – Generally considered easier.
  • Gwanneumsan Trail: (~8.7 km, 5 hours one way to summit) – More challenging but offers dramatic scenery.

What Do I Need for Hallasan Winter Hiking?

Essential gear includes crampons (아이젠), hiking poles, warm layers, waterproof outer shells, gloves, a hat, and plenty of water/snacks. The weather can change rapidly, so preparation is key for a safe and enjoyable experience.

2. Enchanting Camellia Spots in Jeju

Contrast the snowy peaks with Jeju's winter flowers! The island is famous for its vibrant camellia flowers (동백꽃), which bloom from late November to early March.

Best Camellia Viewing Spots for 2025

  • Camellia Hill (동백 포레스트): A large botanical garden dedicated to camellias. Expect beautiful photo zones and diverse varieties. Located near Andeok-myeon.
  • Weoljeong-ri Beach Camellia Path: A lesser-known spot with camellias blooming right next to the famous turquoise waters. Offers a unique seaside backdrop.
  • Jeju Dongbaek Village (제주동백마을): A charming village setting where you can stroll amongst blooming camellias and enjoy local cafes.

3. Other Winter Activities in Jeju (2025)

  • Jeju Tangerine Picking: Enjoy picking fresh, sweet tangerines at various farms.
  • Coastal Drives: The stunning coastal roads, like Aewol Handam Coastal Walk, offer breathtaking winter sea views.
  • Thermal Spas & Hot Springs: Relax in warm waters after a day of exploration.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

Is it worth visiting Jeju Island in winter?

Absolutely! Jeju offers a unique charm in winter with snow hiking on Hallasan, vibrant camellia fields, and fewer crowds. It's a truly unforgettable experience for nature lovers.

What is the weather like in Jeju in January 2025?

January in Jeju is typically cold, with average temperatures ranging from 3°C to 8°C (37°F to 46°F). Expect occasional snow, especially on Hallasan, and chilly winds along the coast. Proper winter attire is essential.

Do I need to rent a car for winter travel in Jeju?

Renting a car (preferably with 4WD or snow chains if you plan to drive towards Hallasan) is highly recommended for flexibility. However, public transport and local taxis are also available for key attractions.
item.excerpt:encoded: Explore Jeju Island in winter 2025 with this guide to Hallasan snow hiking, enchanting camellia spots, and other unique activities. Discover the magic of Korea's volcanic island.

item.title: Where to Stay in Jeju Winter 2025: Top 3 Hotels for Hikers & Sightseers
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
Planning a winter trip to Jeju Island in 2025? Choosing the right base is crucial, especially when balancing early morning Hallasan snow hikes with relaxed coastal sightseeing. Jeju in winter (December to February) offers dramatic snowy landscapes on the mountain and crisp, beautiful views along the coast.

In this guide, we have selected the top 3 hotels for winter 2025, catering to hikers needing easy mountain access and sightseers looking for luxury and convenience. These selections are based on real data regarding location practicality, winter amenities, and traveler reviews.

Read also: Jeju Island Winter Travel Guide 2025: Hallasan Snow Hiking & Camellia Spots

Where is the Best Area to Stay in Jeju in Winter?

Generally, **Seogwipo (South)** is slightly warmer and less windy than **Jeju City (North)** in winter. For Hallasan hikers, staying near major bus routes to trailheads (Seongpanak or Gwanneumsa) is vital. For general sightseers, the **Jungmun Tourist Complex** offers luxury and indoor activities, while the **East (near Seongsan)** is great for sunrise views and coastal drives.

1. The Hiker’s Haven (Best for Hallasan Access): Hotel Bridgene Jeju

If your main goal is hiking Hallasan's snowy peak, convenience is key. The **Seongpanak trail** is the most popular winter route. Staying in Seogwipo City near the main bus terminal makes catching the early morning 281 bus to the trailhead incredibly easy.

Why stay here in Winter 2025?

  • Location: Located centrally in Seogwipo. It is a short walk to bus stops that connect directly to the Hallasan Seongpanak trailhead, eliminating the need for an expensive long-distance taxi before dawn.
  • Comfort for Recovery: It offers clean, modern rooms with comfortable beds—essential for recovering after an 8-hour hike.
  • Nearby Amenities: Surrounded by local restaurants and convenience stores to stock up on hiking snacks and enjoy a hearty dinner post-hike.
  • Value: Excellent price-to-quality ratio for budget-conscious hikers.

2. The Luxury Sightseer’s Escape: Parnas Hotel Jeju

For those who want to enjoy Jeju's winter scenery in ultimate comfort, the **Jungmun Tourist Complex** is ideal. **Parnas Hotel Jeju** stands out for its dramatic cliffside location and exceptional winter amenities.

Why stay here in Winter 2025?

  • Heated Infinity Pool: The highlight is the stunning outdoor heated infinity pool, the longest in Korea. Swimming while overlooking the winter ocean is an unforgettable experience.
  • Ocean Views: Almost all areas of the hotel offer breathtaking, panoramic views of the rugged winter coastline.
  • Indoor Attractions: Located in Jungmun, you are close to indoor spots like museums and botanical gardens, perfect for escaping cold windy days.

3. The Trendy Explorer's Base (East Jeju): Playce Camp Jeju

If you want to explore the eastern side of the island, including **Seongsan Ilchulbong (Sunrise Peak)** and nearby **camellia habitats**, **Playce Camp Jeju** offers a unique, vibrant atmosphere targeting younger travelers.

Why stay here in Winter 2025?

  • Proximity to Sunrise: It's located very close to Seongsan Ilchulbong, making it easy to catch the famous New Year's or winter sunrise.
  • Vibrant Atmosphere: More than just a hotel, it features on-site cafes, pubs, and pop-up stores, providing a fun environment even on chilly winter evenings.
  • Winter Camellia Access: A great base for driving to eastern camellia hotspots like Jeju Dongbaek Arboretum.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) About Jeju Winter Stays

Should I stay in Jeju City or Seogwipo in winter?

For a slightly warmer climate and easier access to most tourist attractions, **Seogwipo (South)** is generally preferred in winter. However, **Jeju City (North)** is better for late arrivals near the airport and access to the Gwanneumsa trail on Hallasan.

Do hotels in Jeju have good heating in winter?

Yes, virtually all hotels and guesthouses in Jeju are well-equipped with modern heating systems, including "ondol" (floor heating) or central heating, ensuring a warm stay even during cold spells.

Is it necessary to rent a car for a winter trip?

While highly recommended for flexibility, especially for sightseeing, it's not strictly necessary. If your main focus is hiking Hallasan, staying near a major bus route works well. If driving in winter, requesting snow tires or chains is crucial if heading into the mountains.
item.excerpt:encoded: Looking for the best place to stay in Jeju for winter 2025? Discover our top 3 hotel picks perfectly suited for Hallasan hikers and sightseers, from budget-friendly bases to luxury escapes with heated pools.

item.title: Hwacheon Sancheoneo Ice Festival 2026: Official Guide, Tickets & Tips
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
Imagine walking on a frozen river with thousands of people, fishing for mountain trout through a hole in the ice. This is the Hwacheon Sancheoneo Ice Festival, selected by CNN as one of the "7 Winter Wonders of the World."

Scheduled to return in January 2026, this festival is the highlight of winter in Korea. This guide covers everything from transportation from Seoul to the exclusive foreigner fishing zones.

Read also: Best Ski Resorts Near Seoul for 2025-2026 Winter

What is the Hwacheon Sancheoneo Ice Festival?

Held in Hwacheon-gun, Gangwon-do, the coldest area in South Korea, this festival attracts over a million visitors annually. The main attraction is catching "Sancheoneo" (mountain trout), a fish that lives only in pristine, fresh water.

Top 3 Must-Do Activities

1. Ice Fishing (Foreigner Zone Recommended)

While the general fishing area is crowded, there is a designated "Foreigner Only Zone". It is less crowded, and rumor has it that the staff release more fish here to ensure international visitors catch at least one!

2. Bare-Handed Fishing

Are you brave enough? Change into a t-shirt and shorts and jump into the icy pool to catch trout with your bare hands. It is an adrenaline-pumping experience you will never forget.

3. Ice Sledding & Snow Sculptures

If fishing is not your style, enjoy traditional Korean ice sledding or explore the massive indoor ice sculpture plaza featuring world-famous landmarks carved from ice.

How to Get to Hwacheon from Seoul (2026 Update)

Hwacheon is about 2.5 hours away from Seoul. Here are your best options:

  • Official Shuttle Bus (Best for Tourists): During the festival period, shuttle bus packages (often included with entrance tickets) depart from Hongdae, Myeongdong, and Dongdaemun. This is the most stress-free option.
  • ITX Train + Bus: Take the ITX-Cheongchun train from Yongsan Station to Namchuncheon Station or Chuncheon Station. Then, take an intercity bus to Hwacheon Terminal (approx. 50 mins).

2026 Festival Tips

  • Dress Code: It is freezing. Wear thermal underwear (Heattech), waterproof boots, and hot packs (kailo).
  • Cooking Your Catch: You can take your caught fish to the nearby "Grill Center." For a small fee (approx. 3,000 KRW), they will grill it fresh for you to eat right there.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

Is the bare-handed fishing event too cold?

Yes, it is intensely cold! However, the event is short (about 3-5 minutes), and there is a warm foot bath and warm clothes changing area prepared immediately after the event to help you recover.

Is there a specific fishing zone for foreigners?

Yes. The Foreigner Only Fishing Zone is located near the upstream area. You usually need to show your passport or ARC (Alien Registration Card) to purchase tickets for this zone.

When is the best time to visit?

Weekdays are highly recommended. Weekends (Saturday/Sunday) are extremely crowded with local tourists. If you must go on a weekend, aim to arrive by 9:00 AM.
item.excerpt:encoded: The ultimate guide to the Hwacheon Sancheoneo Ice Festival 2026. Discover CNN's "Winter Wonder" with tips on ice fishing, foreigner-only zones, and shuttle buses from Seoul.

item.title: Best Christmas Light Spots in Seoul 2025: Shinsegae & Myeongdong Walking Guide
item.dc:creator: duja0518
item.content:encoded:
If you are in Seoul right now (December 2025), there is one thing you absolutely must do tonight: see the Christmas lights in Myeongdong. The holiday displays in Seoul have evolved into massive digital art shows, with Shinsegae Department Store leading the trend.

Here is your ultimate walking guide to the best Christmas light spots in Seoul for 2025, including the best photo zones and timing tips.

Read also: Top 5 Winter Street Foods in Myeongdong You Must Eat

1. The Main Event: Shinsegae Department Store (Main Branch)

This is arguably the most famous Christmas spot in Korea. The entire facade of the building transforms into a massive screen playing a magical winter movie.

  • Theme (2025): "Magical Holiday Journey" (A whimsical train ride through a snowy village).
  • Best Photo Spot: Across the street, in front of the Seoul Central Post Office. Do not try to stand right under the building; you need distance to capture the whole screen.
  • Getting There: Hoehyeon Station (Line 4), Exit 7. Walk towards Myeongdong.

2. The Classic Vibe: Myeongdong Cathedral

After the dazzling lights of Shinsegae, walk up the hill to Myeongdong Cathedral. The vibe here is calm, spiritual, and romantic. The LED rose garden and the life-sized nativity scene offer a quieter, beautiful backdrop.

3. Bonus Spot: Lotte Department Store (Sogong-dong)

Located just a 5-minute walk from Shinsegae, Lotte Department Store features elaborate window displays (animatronics) and a giant Christmas tree. It feels like a classic European Christmas market.

Recommended Walking Route (Approx. 1 Hour)

To minimize crowd stress, follow this route:

  1. Start at Myeongdong Station (Exit 8).
  2. Walk to Myeongdong Cathedral (Photos: 15 mins).
  3. Walk through the Myeongdong street food alley to Lotte Department Store (Photos: 15 mins).
  4. Walk underground via the shopping center to Shinsegae Department Store (Photos: 30 mins).

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

What time do the Christmas lights turn on in Seoul?

For 2025, the lights at Shinsegae and Lotte Department Stores typically turn on at 5:30 PM and turn off at 10:30 PM. Myeongdong Cathedral lights usually stay on until midnight.

Is it crowded? How long is the wait?

Yes, it is extremely crowded, especially on weekends and Christmas Eve. Police and safety staff control the crowd. You might wait 15 to 30 minutes in line just to get to the front row of the photo zone near the Post Office.

When does the Christmas display end?

The displays usually run until January 31, 2026. However, the crowds are thinnest in early January, so visiting after Christmas Day is a smart hack.
item.excerpt:encoded: Explore the best Christmas light spots in Seoul for 2025. A complete walking guide to Shinsegae Department Store's media facade and Myeongdong Cathedral.
